step1.title: Welcome to AgroGina! 🌾
step1.content: Your complete farm management platform. This quick tour shows you the main areas — it takes less than a minute.

step2.title: Navigation
step2.content: Everything is organized in this sidebar: your land, your team, your production, and your finances. We'll walk through each layer.

step3.title: Your Organization
step3.content: Switch between organizations if you manage multiple farms or cooperatives. All data is isolated per organization.

step4.title: Your Account
step4.content: Access your profile, preferences, language settings, and logout from here.

step1.title: The Complete Tour 🌾
step1.content: AgroGina follows your farm's natural workflow: Define your land → Work it → Grow & monitor → Sell & account. Let's see how each module fits.

step2.title: Your Organization
step2.content: Everything starts here. Switch between farms or cooperatives — each has its own data, team, and settings.

step3.title: Dashboard — Your Command Center
step3.content: See key metrics at a glance: parcel health, urgent tasks, and production stats. This is where you start your day.

step4.title: 🌍 Layer 1: Farms
step4.content: Start by defining your farms. A farm groups parcels, workers, and equipment under one roof. Think of it as your organizational unit.

step5.title: 🌍 Layer 1: Parcels
step5.content: Each farm contains parcels — the actual plots of land. Define their boundaries, soil type, irrigation, and current crop. Parcels are the heart of everything.

step6.title: 🌍 Layer 1: Infrastructure
step6.content: Wells, basins, pumping stations, hangars — register your physical assets. They connect to parcels and appear in maintenance schedules.

step7.title: 👥 Layer 2: Personnel
step7.content: Your team: permanent workers, day laborers, managers. Assign tasks, track hours, calculate pay. Tasks link people to parcels.

step8.title: 📦 Layer 2: Inventory
step8.content: Seeds, fertilizers, plant protection products, equipment. Track stock levels, get low-stock alerts, and record every movement in/out.

step9.title: 🌱 Layer 3: Production
step9.content: Campaigns, crop cycles, harvests, quality control. This is where parcels + tasks + inputs come together into actual production data.

step10.title: ✅ Layer 3: Compliance
step10.content: Certifications (GlobalGAP, Bio, etc.), audits, and corrective actions. All your traceability data feeds into compliance reports automatically.

step11.title: 💰 Layer 4: Sales & Purchases
step11.content: Create quotes, convert to orders, manage suppliers and customers. The commercial side of your operation.

step12.title: 📊 Layer 4: Accounting
step12.content: Invoices, payments, journal entries, chart of accounts — double-entry bookkeeping adapted for agriculture. Connected to sales and production.

step13.title: 🤖 AgromindIA — Your AI Assistant
step13.content: The brain of AgroGina. Ask questions about your farm in natural language. It sees your stocks, tasks, weather, and costs to give actionable advice.

step14.title: 📈 Reports & Analytics
step14.content: Production reports, profitability per parcel, labor costs, financial statements. Export to PDF or Excel.

step15.title: Settings & Profile
step15.content: Configure your organization, manage team access, enable/disable modules, and set your preferences.

step16.title: You're Ready! 🚀
step16.content: Start with Farms → Parcels → Tasks — that's the core workflow. Click the help button (?) anytime to replay a module tour.

step1.title: Key Metrics
step1.content: Your farm stats at a glance — total area, active parcels, harvests, and revenue. These update in real-time from your data.

step2.title: Parcel Health
step2.content: Quick view of your parcels and their current status. Click any parcel to see satellite analysis, tasks, and profitability.

step3.title: Upcoming Tasks
step3.content: Tasks that need attention today. Each task links to a parcel, assigned worker, and required inputs — the core of daily operations.

step1.title: Farm Overview
step1.content: Your farms at a glance — total count, combined area, and import/export options. Farms are the top level of your organizational hierarchy.

step2.title: Create a Farm
step2.content: Add a new farm to get started. You'll then add parcels inside it, assign workers, and begin tracking production.

step3.title: Your Farms
step3.content: Each card is a farm with its parcels and sub-farms. Click to see details, manage parcels, or assign a farm manager.

step1.title: Your Parcels
step1.content: Each parcel represents a plot of land with its crop, area, and health status. Parcels are where production, tasks, and satellite data meet.

step2.title: Filter & Search
step2.content: Filter by farm, crop type, or status to focus on what matters. Useful when managing dozens of parcels across multiple farms.

step3.title: Parcel Actions
step3.content: For each parcel: view satellite imagery, check weather, assign tasks, or analyze profitability. Everything is one click away.

step1.title: Task List
step1.content: All agricultural tasks: irrigation, fertilization, harvest, treatments. Each task links to a parcel and can be assigned to a worker.

step2.title: Calendar View
step2.content: Switch to calendar view to plan your week. See task distribution across time and avoid worker conflicts.

step3.title: Create a Task
step3.content: New task: pick a parcel, assign a worker, set priority and deadline. Tasks with stock inputs will automatically deduct from inventory.

step1.title: Your Team
step1.content: All workers — permanent, day laborers, and sharecroppers. See their assigned tasks, availability, and payment history.

step2.title: Payments
step2.content: Calculate pay based on hours worked, tasks completed, or piece rates. Payments flow into your accounting journal automatically.

step3.title: Add a Worker
step3.content: Register a new team member with their role, contact info, and pay terms. They'll appear in task assignment dropdowns immediately.

step1.title: Welcome to Stock 📦
step1.content: This module covers everything you store: seeds, fertilizers, pesticides, packaging, harvested produce. The four groups (Catalog → Movements → Operations → Insights) follow your daily flow. We'll walk every screen — about 90 seconds.

step2.title: Items — Your product catalog
step2.content: Every product you stock lives here (e.g. "Urée 46%", "Mancozèbe 80%", "Caisse 10kg"). Each item has a unit, optional reorder threshold, FIFO/LIFO valuation, and one or more barcodes. Nothing else works without items, so start here.

step3.title: Suppliers — Who you buy from
step3.content: Register vendors with their tax ID (ICE), payment terms, and default currency. When you record a purchase reception, the supplier auto-links to the invoice and account-payable journal entry.

step4.title: Reception — Record incoming purchases
step4.content: The most-used screen. Pick a supplier, scan/select items, enter qty + cost + batch + expiry → posts a Material Receipt that increases warehouse stock and creates the supplier invoice draft. This is where 80% of stock data enters the system.

step5.title: Quick Entry — Fast mobile entry
step5.content: For field workers and quick consumption logging. Scan a barcode → enter qty → submit. Skips the form-heavy reception flow. Perfect for daily fertilizer issues to a parcel.

step6.title: Stock Entries — Full audit log
step6.content: Every IN, OUT, and TRANSFER lives here with its journal entry, batch, parcel, and crop cycle. Filter by date, type, warehouse, or item. This is the source of truth for compliance audits and traceability.

step7.title: Inventory — Live stock by warehouse
step7.content: What you have, where, right now. Drill into any item to see batch breakdowns, oldest lots, reservations, and per-parcel allocations. Use this when planning a treatment or before placing an order.

step8.title: Stock Take — Physical count reconciliation
step8.content: Do this monthly. The wizard lists expected qty per item, you enter the counted qty, and it auto-creates a Stock Reconciliation entry for any variance — no manual math, full audit trail.

step9.title: Expiry Alerts — Compliance + waste prevention
step9.content: Surfaces every batch nearing or past expiry, grouped by urgency (critical / warning / attention). Critical for pesticide compliance — using an expired phytosanitary disqualifies you from GlobalGAP / Bio audits.

step10.title: Aging — Where your money is sleeping
step10.content: Bucketed view of remaining stock value by 0-30 / 31-60 / 61-90 / 90+ days. The 90+ bucket is where you find the 800-DH bottle of pesticide that's been gathering dust since last campaign. Free up cash by acting on it.

step11.title: Ready to start 🚀
step11.content: Your first action: open **Catalog → Items** and create your top 5–10 inputs. Then **Catalog → Suppliers** for your main vendors. Then **Movements → Reception** for your first purchase. Replay this tour anytime via the ? button (top-right).

step1.title: Welcome to Accounting 📊
step1.content: Double-entry bookkeeping adapted to Moroccan agriculture (CGNC chart of accounts, MAD currency, fiscal-year periods). The five groups (Overview → Billing → Records → Reports → Settings) cover the full audit cycle. About 2 minutes for the full tour.

step2.title: Invoices — Sales and purchases
step2.content: Customer and supplier invoices. Posting an invoice creates a balanced journal entry automatically and updates AR/AP. Includes credit notes (avoir) for refunds and returns — visible inline on each posted invoice.

step3.title: Quotes — Pre-sale offers
step3.content: Send quotes to customers (devis). Convert to a sales order with one click when accepted, then to an invoice on delivery. Used heavily for harvest pre-sales to brokers.

step4.title: Payments — Cash in and out
step4.content: Record payments received from customers and made to suppliers. Allocate one payment across multiple invoices. Linked to a bank account; updates AR/AP and cash balance simultaneously.

step5.title: Journal — Every entry, ever
step5.content: The full general journal. Most entries are auto-generated by invoices, payments, stock movements, and harvests. Manual entries (corrections, accruals, depreciation) live here too. Closed periods are read-only.

step6.title: Chart of Accounts — Your CoA
step6.content: Hierarchical CGNC chart pre-seeded for Morocco (311 Marchandises, 612 Achats consommés, 441 Fournisseurs, etc.). Add your own sub-accounts. Every journal item references one account.

step7.title: Bank Accounts — Cash positions
step7.content: Each bank or cash account with its currency and live balance. Payments link here. Reconciliation against bank statements (planned phase 4) will use this list.

step8.title: Profit & Loss — How you're doing
step8.content: Income statement for any period: revenue minus expenses by account. Filter by fiscal year, by farm/parcel, or by crop cycle. The agri-specific view: P&L per parcel using cost-center allocation.

step9.title: Balance Sheet — What you own and owe
step9.content: Snapshot at a date: assets (stock, cash, receivables, biological assets at fair value), liabilities (payables, loans), equity. Required for bank loan applications and SIAM expo audits.

step10.title: Aged Receivables — Who owes you
step10.content: Customers grouped by how late they are: 0-30 / 31-60 / 61-90 / 90+ days. The 90+ bucket = your collections priority list. Same for Aged Payables (what you owe suppliers, by age).

step11.title: Settings — Wire it up once
step11.content: **Account Mappings** tell the system which accounts to debit/credit for each transaction type (auto-seeded for Morocco). **Cost Centers** allocate costs to farms/parcels/cycles. **Fiscal Years** open/close the year and lock prior periods.

step12.title: First moves 🚀
step12.content: If your CoA looks empty, run **Settings → Account Mappings → Seed defaults** first. Then **Billing → Invoices → Create** for your first sale. Watch the journal entry appear automatically. Replay this tour anytime via ? (top-right).

step1.title: Satellite Imagery
step1.content: See your parcels from space. NDVI shows crop vigor, NDWI shows water stress. Detect problems before they're visible in the field.

step2.title: Vegetation Indices
step2.content: Compare different indices side by side. Each tells a different story — vegetative health, moisture, chlorophyll concentration.

step3.title: Time Travel
step3.content: Navigate through historical imagery to see how your parcels evolved. Spot trends, compare seasons, and validate treatment effects.

step1.title: Reports Center
step1.content: All your reports in one place: production, finance, labor, and profitability. Each report pulls live data from across all modules.

step2.title: Export
step2.content: Download as PDF or Excel. Share with your team, your bank, or your certification auditor.

step3.title: Filters & Periods
step3.content: Customize by date range, farm, parcel, or crop. Compare this season to last season to measure improvement.

step1.title: Welcome to Production 🌾
step1.content: This whole section turns parcels + tasks + inputs into actual production data. The 7 sub-pages are organized in time-order: Campaigns set the season → Crop Cycles plant + grow → Harvest pulls fruit out → Reception/Quality grades it → Biological Assets value the trees → Product Applications track inputs used. ~90 seconds.

step2.title: Campaigns — The season container
step2.content: A campaign groups everything for one growing season (e.g. "Citrus 2026-2027"). It can span 1-2 fiscal years and links farms, parcels, costs, revenues. Open it to see total area, planned vs actual production, and budgets per cycle.

step3.title: Crop Cycles — Plant to harvest
step3.content: Each cycle is one planting on one parcel inside a campaign. Track planting date, expected/actual harvest dates, status (planned → land prep → growing → harvesting → completed), and IFRS-41 valuation method. The bridge between agronomy and accounting.

step4.title: Harvests — Record what comes out
step4.content: Log harvest events: parcel + crop + quantity + quality grade + estimated revenue. Each record updates your production stats and is the starting point for traceability — every harvest gets a batch number.

step5.title: Reception Batches — At the warehouse
step5.content: When the harvest arrives at the packing house: weigh, sort, assign batch numbers. Tracks shrinkage, calibration, and the link to compliance audit trails (GlobalGAP, Bio require this).

step6.title: Quality Control — Per-batch grading
step6.content: Quality inspections per batch: defects, calibration, certification compliance. A failed inspection can downgrade a batch or trigger corrective action. All scores feed the compliance module.

step7.title: Biological Assets — Trees, livestock, plants
step7.content: IFRS 41 fair-value tracking for productive assets: bearer plants (citrus, olive, almond), livestock. Records acquisition, maturity, depreciation, and unrealized gains/losses on revaluation. Required for proper farm balance sheets.

step8.title: Product Applications — What you applied where
step8.content: Every fertilizer, pesticide, or treatment applied to a parcel: product, dose, area, date, worker. Pulls from stock (deducts inventory), feeds compliance (PPP usage logs), and contributes to per-parcel cost. Replay this tour anytime via ? (top-right).

step1.title: Your Infrastructure
step1.content: Wells, basins, pumping stations, hangars, offices. Each links to a farm and can have maintenance schedules.

step2.title: Add Infrastructure
step2.content: Register a new asset: type, location, capacity, and commissioning date. Maintenance reminders keep your equipment running.

step1.title: Billing Overview
step1.content: Quotes pending, orders to deliver, invoices to collect. The commercial pipeline that turns your production into revenue.

step2.title: Quotes
step2.content: Create professional quotes for buyers. When accepted, convert to a sales order with one click.

step3.title: Orders
step3.content: Sales orders and purchase orders. Track deliveries and receipts. Orders generate invoices and update your accounting.

step4.title: Invoices
step4.content: Issue invoices and track payments. Overdue invoices show in your dashboard and can trigger automatic reminders.

step5.title: Contacts
step5.content: Your customers, suppliers, and transporters. Contact history and transaction records in one place.

step1.title: Settings Menu
step1.content: All configuration options organized by category. Start with Organization to set up your farm details.

step2.title: Organization
step2.content: Your farm's identity: name, address, logo, currency, and fiscal year. This information appears on invoices and reports.

step3.title: Team Members
step3.content: Invite team members and assign roles: admin, farm manager, agronomist, worker, or viewer. Each role sees different features.

step4.title: Subscription
step4.content: Your current plan and features. Upgrade to unlock more parcels, satellite analysis, or advanced AI recommendations.

step5.title: Modules
step5.content: Enable only what you need: accounting, satellite, compliance, fruit trees. Disabled modules are hidden from the sidebar.

step6.title: Your Profile
step6.content: Personal preferences: language (French, English, Arabic), theme, notification settings. Your settings follow you across devices.

et.description: Evapotranspiration (ET) is the total water loss from land to the atmosphere, combining evaporation (water turning into vapor from soil, water bodies, or wet surfaces) and transpiration (water released by plants through their leaves). ET₀ (reference ET) is key for irrigation planning.
et.irrigationInsight: ET₀ represents the water demand of the atmosphere. Multiply ET₀ by the crop coefficient (Kc) to estimate actual crop water needs (ETc = ET₀ × Kc). Higher ET₀ values indicate greater irrigation requirements.
